title: Rom Mohc
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Mohc_Star_Wars_TCG.png]] |name=Rom Mohc |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],{{JournalCite|6|Galaxywide NewsNets}} places the outbreak of the [[Candorian plague/Legends|Candorian plague]] on [[Dentaal]] on 36:2:12, which falls in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. {{FactFile|130|MAD 1-4|General Crix Madine}} tie together the events of ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' and [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], and Madine's own recollections in [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']] as follows: upon learning of the [[Dark Trooper Project]], Madine alerts the Rebel Alliance to the project's existence, thus starting the events of ''Dark Forces''. Shortly afterwards, Madine is ordered to release the Candorian plague on Dentaal, an event which is dated to 36:2:12 per the Adventure Journal. Madine vanishes into the wilderness shortly afterwards, spending a week in the jungles of Dentaal before escaping and fleeing the planet—only to be captured almost immediately by General Rom Mohc and imprisoned on [[Orinackra]]. Kyle Katarn then frees Madine from Orinackra, and Madine is then involved in the [[Battle of Corellia (1 ABY)|battle]] at [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that only a short amount of time passed between Madine telling the Alliance about the Dark Troopers and his defection. The length of time Madine spends on the run places his capture and imprisonment on Orinackra firmly in the month 36:3, or the first month of [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of ''Dark Forces'' prior to the [[mission to Orinackra]] fall in 0 ABY, and the remainder of the game occur in 1 ABY. ''[[Arc Hammer]]''''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Atrisian]]}}{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} **[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]]''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' **[[Dark Trooper Project]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|It's been a long time since I've challenged a man to battle. I'm glad my opponent is so worthy.|General Mohc prepares to face Rebel agent [[Kyle Katarn]]|audio=GladMyOpponentIsWorthy-DF.flac|}} [[General/Legends|General]] '''Rom Mohc''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was a hero of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], serving in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and later the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Having developed an obsession with cybernetics and personal combat during the Clone Wars, Mohc was the architect of the [[Dark Trooper Project]] that arose in the wake of the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]. Despite initial successes in field testing, the project was destroyed by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agent [[Kyle Katarn]], who defeated Mohc in single combat. ==Biography== ===Early career=== Rom Mohc was born in the insular culture of [[Atrisia/Legends|Atrisia]], and was one of the few [[Atrisian]]s who excelled off-world.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' A non-[[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Republic officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], Rom Mohc fought in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] under the command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Locus Geen]]. A [[cybernetics/Legends|cybernetics]] specialist, Mohc gained a reputation for fighting [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] in hand-to-hand combat and proved invaluable during the [[Coruscant Insurrection|Invasion of Coruscant]] by the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].''[[Star Wars: The New Droid Army]]'' Mohc's bravery during the Clone Wars made him a celebrated hero over the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]], and his loyalty put him on the fast track to success in [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. On many worlds, Mohc fought in Palpatine's name, earning three [[Medal of Valor/Legends|Imperial Medals of Valor]] and the [[Distinguished Medal of Imperial Honor/Legends|Distinguished Medal of Imperial Honor]]. Mohc continued his fascination with droids, often pitting himself against various deadly droids, including a prototype [[IG-97 battle droid|IG-97]], a [[ZQ Infantry Support Unit]], and several [[Mark IX-XI Executioner]]s. His eccentric taste for combat culminated in a trophy room of battle droids that he had defeated. The [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star project]] was something that was against his martial philosophy and career he was following, and was one of those who protested against it.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' ===Battle droid research=== Mohc found his inspiration while fighting as a [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper]], and came to believe more and more that the ultimate warbot could be built. He started an extensive historical and technological research, based on the designs of mechanical soldiers, from the [[Pre-Republic era|Pre-Republic]] [[war-robot]]s of [[Xim/Legends|Xim]] and the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] to the more recent Republic [[Iron Knight]]s. Mohc's research yielded the initial designs for what would become the [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]]. Allying with [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]], Mohc designed the [[Z-X3 experimental droid trooper]], designed to supplement [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] in dangerous radiation or vacuum. An experimental batch of seventy droids proved deadly and adaptable, but the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] viewed the use of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] with distaste and the Z-X3 was rejected. Mohc refused to admit defeat and kept pushing his ideas. His perseverance finally paid off when he won support from the [[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]], and in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]] young [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Mohc was stationed aboard the ''[[Empirical]]'', a hidden research vessel in the [[Dominus sector/Legends|Dominus]] sector that belonged to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], to oversee the [[Phase Zero dark trooper|Phase Zero Dark Trooper]] project, an attempt to restore aging [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to battle-worthiness by remaking them into [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]]. However, as the subjects were forcibly recruited into the project, many Phase Zero Dark Troopers could not cope with the state of being more machine than man and attempted suicide. The project was eventually shut down as the Empire grew in power and the recruitment rate of non-clones rose, though his successful [[Purge trooper|Purge Troopers]] droids would later inspire the design of the [[Phase III Dark Trooper|Phase III dark trooper]]. ===Imperial general=== {{Quote|The Emperor spent more than a half-million credits to feed, house, and educate each one of you over the past four years. Not because he thought it would be the nice thing to do or because he likes military parades, but because he wants you to ''defend'' the Empire.|General Mohc oversees graduating cadets|''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''}} [[File:RomMohc-DFRemaster.jpg|thumb|left|250px|General Rom Mohc of the Galactic Empire]] Having been a prominent figure during the Empire's rise to power, Mohc was eventually promoted to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[General/Legends|general]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|military forces]],{{CSWECite|II|323|Mohc, General Rom}} where [[Gender/Legends|he]] oversaw the Imperial [[Dark Trooper Project]].''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' During the year [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]'' during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. General Mohc worked as an [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] at the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy of the]] [[Carida/Legends|planet Carida]]. During his term, Mohc installed a high-tech surveillance system meant to act as a deterrent for misbehavior among cadets.[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'' audio drama]] Even so, General Mohc was well-respected by the academy's students due to his status as a combat veteran and not just another [[Officer/Legends|officer]]. Mohc was particularly impressed with cadet [[Kyle Katarn]], who acted with bravery on his [[Omega Exercise]], which involved the [[Battle of AX-456|taking]] of [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[AX-456]] from the forces of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. To honor the the cadet's bravery, Mohc personally commended Katarn at the latter's bedside. Later that same [[Standard day/Legends|day]], General Mohc hosted dinner for the [[High Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] (who had executed Katarn's [[Morgan Katarn|father]] sometime earlier for being an Alliance militant) and expressed his interest in meeting the cadet. The two Imperials later, using Mohc's surveillance system, spied on Katarn as he shared his [[Force-sensitive/Legends|strange experiences]] during the mission, to [[Meck Odom]]. Mohc later addressed the year's graduating class, where he was given the cadets' full attention. After the speech's conclusion, and according to Jerec's wish to meet Katarn, the General and Jerec presented awards to the students who graduated in the top ten percent of the class, which included presenting Katarn with the [[Medal of Honor (Imperial)/Legends|Imperial Medal of Honor]]. Special awards of valor and merit were also distributed to cadets [[Rand Nalleck]], [[Dana Cory]], [[Chad Longko]], and [[Ben (Imperial cadet)|Ben]]. However, as the ceremony progressed, Mohc wished for it to end so he could entertain a mistress. Despite the Imperials' praise for Katarn, the cadet abandoned the Empire upon realizing that he had been lied to about the circumstances of his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] [[Death/Legends|death]], having been told rebel insurgents killed his father rather than being executed by an Imperial official. Katarn joined the Rebel Alliance and quickly partook in a mission where he [[Operation Skyhook|stole information regarding the Death Star project]] that helped the Alliance destroy the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|battle station]] during the year [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction of the Death Star]] during [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. Ironically, General Mohc had previously opposed the construction of the Death Star due to his distrusting nature of technology that separated soldiers from battlefield glory. ===The Dark Trooper Project=== {{Quote|The Emperor is most pleased with your project.|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|{{Tales|4|Sand Blasted}}}} Ever since his promotion to the rank of [[General/Legends|general]], Mohc used the resources available to him to complete the technical designs and implementation plans for his own dark troopers, thinking that infantry units should prove themselves in battle. He believed his creation would please the Emperor, but the latter was obsessed with [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin's]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] project. He was one of the few [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] to oppose the Death Star as Mohc had a distrust of any technology which separated warriors from their enemies in battle. With the failure of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Mohc seized the opportunity to pitch his program to Palpatine. Infuriated with the late [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] Tarkin's failure and recognizing the partial success of the Phase Zero project, the Emperor granted General Mohc authorization to begin his new program. The Dark Trooper Project was given billions of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to support the research and development of Mohc's brainchild. Mohc established a mining facility on [[Gromas 16]] to acquire the near-indestructible metal [[Phrik/Legends|phrik]], a weapons research facility on [[Fest/Legends|Fest]], a robotics construction facility on [[Anteevy/Legends|Anteevy]], and the massive starship ''[[Arc Hammer]]'' to act as a construction and mobile launch base for the dark troopers. ====Test on Talay==== {{Quote|Captain Nonam, are your ground troops standing by?''"
"''They await your order, sir. The Rebel scum are ''doomed''.|General Mohc and Captain Nonam during the attack on Tak Base|}} [[File:RomMohc2.jpg|thumb|right|170px|Darth Vader himself approved Mohc's dark trooper Project.]] During the year [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], General Mohc met with [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth Vader]] aboard the latter's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Executor/Legends|Executor]],'' in order to officially test the dark troopers with a combat scenario. A Rebel base was located on the planet [[Talay/Legends|Talay]]: [[Tak Base]]. Mohc gave the order to unleash phase II dark troopers upon the base via [[Hyperdrive pod/Legends|hyperspace pods]], which were reinforced by ground troops under the command of [[Captain]] [[Nonam]]. [[Attack on Tak Base|Tak Base was obliterated]], and all Rebel personnel were killed. In addition, the surrounding [[City/Legends|city]] of Tak was razed, and civilian casualties were high. The dark troopers were subsequently recalled while an Imperial garrison remained in the city. However, one dark trooper left its [[Imperial repeater rifle|prototype weapon]] behind in the Tak Base. Nonetheless, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader was impressed with the show of force, and General Mohc was given the go-ahead to continue with the project. Despite the destruction, the Rebel officer General [[Toka]] had managed to send the Alliance a distress message before he was killed. Although no footage of the dark troopers was recorded, the video-message did capture the sounds that the droid troopers made. The message was received by Alliance high-command, who in turn dispatched agents [[Jan Ors]] and Kyle Katarn to investigate the battle's aftermath. During the investigation, Katarn discovered the dark trooper weapon. With Rebus' initials on it, the Rebels had a lead, and set out to end Mohc's ambitions. ====A worthy opponent==== {{Quote|Katarn was once an impressive Imperial officer, but he was weak and gave up on the struggle for our new order. I wouldn't put much faith in his abilities.|General Mohc on his adversary|}} Mohc's assembly line began to falter when the Alliance operation began with the [[Mission to Anoat|capture of Moff Rebus]], the dark trooper project's weapon designer, on the planet [[Anoat/Legends|Anoat]], whose subsequent interrogation led to Katarn [[Mission to Fest (Galactic Civil War)|infiltrating]] and [[Mission to Gromas|destroying]] dark trooper facilities on Fest and Gromas 16. With Katarn's actions directly threatening Mohc's operation, the general hired the famed [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to deal with the Rebels. Shortly thereafter, Crix Madine's role as a rebel spy was rooted, and Mohc arrested the traitor before both Vader and Fett aboard the ''Arc Hammer''. Although Mohc's own identity was a mystery to the rebel agents, the Imperials were well aware of Katarn. Mohc even recalled the agent's past Imperial career for those present. Despite Vader's growing doubts about the project, Mohc firmly claimed that Katarn would never near the ''Arc hammer''. [[File:Shrouded_Dark_Trooper.png|thumb|left|200px|Rom Mohc emerges for battle.]] Madine was sentenced to execution and was incarcerated at the [[Imperial Detention Center]] located on the planet [[Orinackra]]. However, he was [[Mission to Orinackra|rescued]] by Katarn only a short while later during the year [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], after which Madine told the Rebels what he knew about Mohc and the dark trooper project. Madine's information resulted in Katarn [[Mission to Cal-Seti|tracking]] Jabba the Hutt's loaned smugglers from the planet [[Cal-Seti/Legends|Cal-Seti]] to Ice Station Beta on Anteevy, which was also [[Mission to Anteevy|destroyed]] by Katarn. The rebels later [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (Dark Trooper campaign)|stole]] a [[nava card]] from the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] which, after [[Mission to Coruscant (Kyle Katarn)|being cracked]] in the [[Imperial Security Operations]] building on Coruscant, revealed information on the routes that the smugglers used. It was during the latter mission that Katarn encountered Fett and, after a heated battle, defeated the bounty hunter. After that, Katarn used the information from the nava card to [[Mission to Fuel Station Ergo|follow the smugglers]] to the ''Executor''. Although Mohc had believed that the ''Arc Hammer'' was beyond Katarn's competencies, Katarn nonetheless [[Mission to the Executor|infiltrated]] the ''Executor'', stole a cargo container, and [[Mission to the Arc Hammer|boarded]] the ''Arc Hammer''. Realizing that Katarn was far more capable than anticipated, Mohc decided to regard the rebel agent as an adversary of merit. In response to rebel infiltration, Mohc donned his phase III dark trooper armor and lay in wait near the ''Arc Hammer''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] for a final confrontation with Katarn. Meanwhile, Katarn fought Mohc's forces and planted an explosive device on three of the ''Arc Hammer''{{'s}} [[exchange coupling]]s that were built along the ship's main power grid. As Katarn made his way to the ship's hangar bay to find an escape, he found the massive ante-chamber where Mohc was waiting. The general only revealed himself, however, after Katarn destroyed the seven dark troopers that guarded the arena. A massive door opened revealing Mohc atop a pedestal and the final battle commenced.''[[Dark Forces Official Player's Guide]]'' The General fought the rebel he had personally commended and decorated on Carida to the point that Katarn had need to only outlast Mohc's barrage of attacks. Ultimately, General Mohc was slain and Katarn escaped the ''Arc Hammer'' before the explosives detonated and destroyed all that Mohc had worked for. ==Personality and traits== Mohc appeared to [[Kyle Katarn]], during his graduation, as a relatively short man with barrel chest, and a [[Dog/Legends|bulldog]] face, traits he had up to his death a couple of years later. Mohc was infamous for his eccentric and violent intelligence, and obsession for personal hand-to-hand combat. His keen understanding of cybernetics gave him the ability to ascertain the weaknesses of various droid designs of the Separatists and use this knowledge against them during combat. Having fought and defeated many battle droids over the years, he made a personal collection of them in his personal trophy room. Mohc's passion for personal combat became almost obsessive, and he came to insist that every soldier had the right to face the enemy man-to-man, to the point of protesting the Death Star project. He had the reputation of a real soldier, who only followed orders, even unpleasant ones. He believed in the authority of the Emperor, the consistency of a single voice, instead of the [[Democracy/Legends|"chaos"]] of [[Galactic Senate/Legends|different opinions]]. He believed that the Empire is attacked from within, and as he said to his graduation speech, the duty of the fledgie officers was to "find the rot, cut it out, and restore [[New Order/Legends|order]]". He ascribed defection as a "weakness", such as defector Kyle Katarn too weak to remain with the Empire. However in his final challenge, he recognised him as a worthy opponent. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Rom_Mohc_Conceptual_Art.png|thumb|right|200px|Conceptual artwork of Rom Mohc.]] Mohc is the main antagonist of the video game ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' and is mainly seen during cutscenes which advance the plot. His voice in ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' was provided by [[Jack Angel]]. [[Tim Russell]] voiced Mohc for the audio adaptation of [[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'']]. Mohc is seen again in the story "[[Sand Blasted]]" which extrapolates on one of the game's cutscenes, but his appearance differs somewhat from that of the game, showing him with receding but long, red hair. In the cancelled ''[[The Clone Wars Sourcebook]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]], Rom Mohc would have clashed with General [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] on "the spike-stone plains of Mendig," with Grievous refusing to kill him in favor of having Mohc return to Coruscant in shame. This encounter would have inspired Mohc's obssession with personal combat and the subsequent creation of the dark troopers.{{Blog|danwallace|35|Lostwords: The Clone Wars Sourcebook}} An [[action figure]] of Mohc was given with one of [[IG-97 battle droid|IG-97]] with a reprint of ''Sand Blasted'' as a part of a "Comic Pack".{{Rebelscum|url=TLCcomicpacksWM101.asp|text=IG97 & ROM MOHC] in
